linkedin.text:
Your funnel math is lying to you.

I sat in a board meeting where the VP of Marketing showed a model: 10,000 visitors → 500 MQLs → 100 SQLs → 25 opps → 5 deals. Clean ratios, tidy conversion rates, a number that "worked."

The CEO approved the budget. Six months later, they had 3 deals, not 5. The model was off by 40%.

Here's what the model missed: it assumed every stage converted independently. But funnel stages aren't independent. Friction compounds. A 2% drop at stage 1 doesn't cost you 2% — it cascades.

I built a calculator that models this properly. And wrote about why the standard funnel math kills deals before they ever become deals.

The post + calculator:
linkedin.title: The Math That Kills the Deal Before the Deal Is a Lead

x.text:
Your funnel math is lying to you.

A 2% drop at the top of your funnel doesn't cost you 2%. It cascades through every stage.

I wrote about why standard B2B funnel models systematically overcount pipeline — and built a calculator to model it properly.

https://strategnik.com/thinking/funnel-math-kills-the-deal/
